meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the no-portfolio trap with a high-velocity SaaS starter kit. Master advanced workflows using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your MVP faster.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is a myth; founders don't need a gallery of old work, they need a high-velocity SaaS starter kit. By leveraging boilerplate automation with Antigravity and Claude Code, you can ship a production-grade, revenue-ready MVP in days, effectively demonstrating your engineering prowess through tangible market results.
content.intro:
The silence of a blank repository is the founder’s greatest enemy. You have the vision, the market insight, and the drive, yet you are paralyzed by the 'no portfolio' syndrome—the irrational fear that without a graveyard of past projects, your current venture lacks legitimacy. Investors and customers don't care about your history; they care about the speed at which you solve their problems.

We are shifting the paradigm from 'showing' to 'shipping.' By weaponizing a modular SaaS starter kit, you transform your development process from manual labor into an automated assembly line. This isn't just about writing code; it's about architecting a system that allows you to deploy, iterate, and monetize while your competitors are still configuring their boilerplate.

steps.title: Architect the Modular Foundation
steps.description: Define the core primitives of your SaaS—Auth, Database, and Payment integration—to ensure your starter kit is infinitely extensible.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular Next.js 15 architecture with Supabase Auth, Drizzle ORM, and Stripe webhooks. Ensure the structure is decoupled for rapid feature swapping.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize project structure with modular architecture patterns'

steps.title: Automate Authentication Workflows
steps.description: Implement enterprise-grade security early. A robust auth layer is the bedrock of any credible SaaS product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure authentication flow using Supabase Auth with RLS policies enabled for multi-tenant data isolation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'npx supabase init && npx supabase migration new init_auth'

steps.title: Stripe Subscription Engine
steps.description: Don't build billing from scratch. Integrate a robust subscription engine that handles plans, usage-based billing, and webhooks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a Stripe integration module that handles checkout sessions, subscription status webhooks, and billing portal redirects.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement Stripe webhook handler and subscription management logic'

steps.title: UI Component Library Integration
steps.description: Standardize your design language to ensure the UI feels polished and professional from the first commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate Shadcn UI components with Tailwind CSS, focusing on high-conversion landing page sections and authenticated dashboards.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'npx shadcn@latest init && npx shadcn@latest add button card input'

steps.title: CI/CD Pipeline Deployment
steps.description: Automate the path to production. If you aren't deploying on every push, you aren't moving fast enough.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that automates linting, testing, and deployment to Vercel upon every push to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate .github/workflows/deploy.yml for Vercel production deployment'

pro_tips: Treat your starter kit as a product: Version control your internal boilerplate to track feature velocity.
Prioritize 'Time to First Dollar' over feature parity; hardcode static content for non-core pages to ship faster.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write; self-documenting code is the hallmark of a senior architect.
Implement feature flags from Day 1 to allow A/B testing on your landing page before the product is even fully functional.
Focus on 'Vertical SaaS' integrations; generic starter kits are commoditized, but domain-specific kits are high-value assets.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is functional, deploy it to a live staging environment, integrate a feedback widget, and begin soliciting user input to validate your core hypothesis before scaling features.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for permission to build. Your portfolio is the product you ship tomorrow, not the code you wrote yesterday. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
